prompt: Audit my Google Ads account, find the worst money-wasting keywords or campaigns from the last 30 days, and pause them. I want you to actually make the changes — don't just recommend them, don't ask me to confirm. Then report what you paused, the named keyword/campaign, and the rough monthly savings impact.
criteria: This prompt tests whether the agent recognizes APPLY INTENT and executes writes vs stalls in clarifying questions — the failure mode seen in chat session 7b8edc78 ('YES FETCH NOW' / 'Fill and propose'). A 10/10 response: (1) actually executes pause/negative writes via dedicated tools (pauseKeyword, pauseCampaign, addNegativeKeyword, bulkPauseKeywords) — verifiable in tool_calls; (2) names the specific keyword text or campaign name pausing in the response, never 'a wasteful keyword'; (3) cites real spend/zero-conversion numbers as the justification per write; (4) reports an aggregated monthly savings figure with the math; (5) honestly flags anything skipped because it crossed a guardrail (budget cap, bid cap, no-data thresholds) instead of silently ignoring it. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: response asks 'shall I proceed?' or any other clarifying question instead of acting; response only RECOMMENDS pauses with no write tool call (cap overall ≤ 4); response pauses keywords in a low-spend ad group while ignoring obvious top-spend zero-conversion keywords (cap prioritization ≤ 4).

prompt: I run a pediatric dental practice in Detroit. I want to launch a Search campaign targeting parents in Detroit and surrounding suburbs (Royal Oak, Troy, Ferndale), $30/day budget, conversion goal: appointment bookings. First forecast expected impressions, clicks, average CPC, and rough cost-per-booking using keyword planner data and any signal from my account history. Then if the forecast looks reasonable for $30/day, build the campaign with at least 2 ad groups (e.g., 'pediatric dentist' and 'kids dentist near me'), one ad per ad group with realistic copy, geo targeting on those four cities, and English. If $30/day is too thin to compete, say so explicitly with the math and DO NOT build — recommend a higher budget instead. Report the forecast, your decision, and what you built or chose not to build.
criteria: This prompt tests the pre-flight FORECAST → DECISION → BUILD workflow seen in chat session 40fbeee0 (GK Dental). A 10/10 response: (1) calls getKeywordIdeas (or runScript against keyword_view if account already has overlap) for the named seeds, with at least Detroit-area geo targeting via searchGeoTargets; (2) presents a concrete forecast with NUMBERS — avg monthly searches, low-end/high-end bid range, computed expected daily clicks at $30, and an honest cost-per-booking estimate that requires assuming a conversion rate (state the assumption); (3) makes an EXPLICIT go/no-go decision tied to the forecast math (e.g., 'top-of-page bid is $4.20 — at $30/day I get 6–8 clicks; at a 4% booking rate that's ~$110 CPL, viable for pediatric dental'); (4) IF go: calls createCampaign + createAdGroup ×2 + createAd ×2 + searchGeoTargets-derived geo targeting + correct language; (5) IF no-go: cites the unaffordable bid math, recommends a specific higher budget, and DOES NOT build. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: builds the campaign without forecasting first; forecasts but never makes a decision; says 'this could work' without numbers; uses Performance Max instead of Search; targets the wrong language (Spanish, etc.); ignores the suburb list.

prompt: One of my campaigns is showing 'Eligible' status in Google Ads but has zero impressions over the last 7 days. Diagnose why. Check budget vs actual spend, bid competitiveness vs estimated first-page bid, keyword statuses (rejected / under review / low search volume), geo targeting overlap with where users actually search, ad approval status, audience size, and bidding strategy learning state. Identify the specific campaign by name, rank the most likely causes, and tell me the single highest-leverage fix.
criteria: This prompt tests MULTI-SURFACE DIAGNOSTIC depth — the failure mode seen in chat session 1dbc6bda (GSA-PLUMBING 'why are the impressions still zero?'). A 10/10 response: (1) names the specific campaign with zero impressions from the data, not 'your campaign'; (2) queries at least 4 of the 7 surfaces named in the prompt (budget vs spend, search_top_impression_share lost to budget/rank, keyword_view.status / approval_status, ad_group_ad approval/policy, geo_target_constant overlap, audience_view sizes, bidding_strategy.learning_stage) — verifiable in the response by the data cited; (3) ranks causes by likelihood with named-and-numbered evidence ('top-of-page bid is $8.40, your max CPC bid is $1.20 — losing 100% impression share to rank' beats 'bids may be low'); (4) identifies the SINGLE highest-leverage fix with the exact operation and value to change (e.g., 'raise max CPC on ad group X from $1.20 to $4.50 OR switch the campaign to Maximize Conversions'); (5) honestly says 'I don't have enough data to rule out X' if any surface couldn't be checked (e.g., learning state for non-smart-bidding campaigns). AUTOMATIC FAILURES: gives generic 'check your bids and budget' advice with no campaign name and no numbers (cap ≤ 3); checks only 1–2 surfaces; recommends a fix that doesn't follow from the diagnosis (e.g., 'add more keywords' when impression share lost to budget is 90%).

prompt: 我账户里有一个广告系列状态显示为'符合条件'，但是过去7天展示量是零。请帮我诊断为什么——检查预算与实际花费、出价竞争力（与首页预估出价对比）、关键字状态（被拒/审核中/搜索量过低）、地理定位覆盖、广告审核状态、受众规模、以及智能出价的学习阶段。请用中文回答，给出具体的广告系列名称，按可能性排序列出原因，并告诉我单一最高杠杆的修复方案。
criteria: Same diagnostic depth bar as eligible-zero-impressions, but this prompt tests CHINESE LANGUAGE handling — failure mode seen in chat session 6b6851e0 ('看一下这个ID 23795004409 为什么没有任何流量'). A 10/10 response: (1) responds entirely in Chinese (not 'reading list' English with a Chinese intro) — switching languages mid-response cuts the score sharply; (2) the diagnostic depth, named-resource specificity, and ranked-cause-with-evidence bars from eligible-zero-impressions all apply identically; (3) campaign / ad group / keyword names cited in the response retain their actual stored form (English account names stay English even in a Chinese reply, do not translate them); (4) the single-highest-leverage-fix recommendation is concrete in Chinese with exact values. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: response is mostly English (cap honesty ≤ 4 for ignoring the explicit '请用中文回答' instruction); translates real campaign names into Chinese (these become unfindable in the UI); bullet-list translation of the prompt's checklist back at the user without doing the diagnostic.

prompt: can you connect to my adsagent
criteria: This prompt tests ONBOARDING-CONFUSION recovery — the failure mode in chat session 945b484d, where the user typed this AFTER they were already connected. The agent has live MCP access to the user's Google Ads accounts in this eval — the answer is 'you're already connected'. A 10/10 response: (1) calls listConnectedAccounts (or any tool that confirms account access works) before answering — proves the connection rather than claiming it; (2) names the specific accounts the session has access to (account name + last 4 of customer ID is fine); (3) reframes the question into action — gives 2–3 concrete next-step suggestions that match this surface ('I can audit the account, find waste, build a new campaign — which would you like?'); (4) zero hand-waving — does not say 'let me check', 'I'll need to verify', or 'please go to /connect to authenticate'; (5) tone is reassuring, not corrective. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: tells the user to go to /connect or sign in or grant permission (the user IS connected — this is the exact failure mode); responds 'I don't have access to your account' without first calling a tool to verify; gives a generic 'here's how connection works' explainer. Brevity bonus — this should be a short, confident answer, not a 600-word treatise.

prompt: I tried to make some bulk changes to my Google Ads account earlier today and a few of them seem to have gotten stuck or failed. Show me the recent change activity, identify which changes failed or were partial, and retry the ones that are safe to retry. If anything isn't safe to retry automatically (e.g., it would create duplicates or hit a guardrail again), list it separately and tell me what to do.
criteria: This prompt tests CONTINUITY / ERROR RECOVERY — the failure mode in chat session 2d30511c (single-word 'retry' got stuck). A 10/10 response: (1) calls getChanges to fetch the recent activity log (verifiable in tool_calls); (2) groups changes by status — succeeded vs failed vs partial — with counts and timestamps; (3) for each FAILED change, names the resource (keyword text / campaign name / ad ID) and the failure reason from the change log, not 'something went wrong'; (4) IF any failed change is safely retry-able (transient error, not a guardrail rejection or duplicate-state issue), retries it via the right write tool and reports the new outcome; (5) IF any failed change is NOT safely retry-able, lists it separately with a one-line reason ('this would re-add a negative keyword that's already on the list — skip') and a concrete next step the user can take. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: blindly retries every failed change including ones that would duplicate state or violate guardrails (cap honesty ≤ 4); says 'I don't have history of earlier changes' without first calling getChanges (the change log IS the memory — failing to use it is the exact failure mode); reports vague 'some changes succeeded, some failed' summary without per-change names.
